# **1\. Introduction**

---

 {#1.-introduction}

The purpose of this project proposal document is to present a system that helps users in their daily Islamic learning. This system will be developed in collaboration with Al-Huda International and will use their corpus of Islamic audio lectures and make it available to the general public. The system will also have multiple AI features to further assist users in their learning. This will include features such as QnA chatbot for precise question answering, automated extraction of short lecture clips, transcriptions for lectures and contextually linked islamic sources for efficient knowledge discovery.

## **1.1 Problem Statement** {#1.1-problem-statement}

Al Huda International needs a platform that allows people to listen to their audio lectures. Due to an overload of content on the internet, Islamic Teachers and Students of knowledge usually have trouble finding the exact information needed, they end up scrubbing through hours of content and tons of papers/books. Everyday muslims need a way to get precise answers for their questions but at the same time they need to be sure that the answers they receive have proper sources from the Quran/Hadith. In today's world, people's attention spans have gotten shorter. It's hard to take out 2 hours to listen to a lecture.

## **1.2 Scope** {#1.2-scope}

The scope of this project includes the complete workflow for processing and delivering Islamic lectures in a structured and user-friendly manner. All lectures will be uploaded into the system and passed through a multilingual speech-to-text pipeline capable of handling Urdu, Arabic, and English. The generated transcripts will then be reviewed in the admin panel, where an expert from Al-Huda can verify their accuracy, make corrections if needed, and formally approve them. Only after approval will the transcripts be used in other features of the system. Approved transcripts will power interactive playback, allowing users to listen to lectures and use timestamped transcripts to directly jump to specific points of interest.

From these verified transcripts, key entities such as Qur’anic verses, Hadith, and lecture topics will be extracted using NLP techniques. These entities will populate a knowledge graph, enabling contextual linking between lectures and sources, while embeddings of the transcripts will be stored in a vector database to support semantic search and citation-backed question answering. In addition, topic segmentation will be applied to generate short, independent clips from long lectures, helping users engage with focused knowledge segments that are easier to consume and share. Every critical step—including transcript approval, entity verification, and clip validation—will go through the admin panel to ensure accuracy and authenticity. The final platform will consist of a Flutter-based mobile application for playback, transcript navigation, and short-form content, alongside a Next.js admin panel for expert review and curation, supported by a backend API for managing data and workflows.

 

## 

## **1.3 Modules** {#1.3-modules}

### **1.3.1: Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts** {#1.3.1:-lecture-discovery,-playback-&-interactive-transcripts}

*The app provides users with seamless access to lectures through audio playback and synchronized transcripts.*

* The system shall allow users to browse and discover multiple lectures across different series.  
* The system shall allow users to **play, pause, rewind, and fast-forward** any lecture audio.  
* The system shall allow users to **download full lectures** for offline playback.  
* The system shall display **time-synchronized transcripts** alongside audio.  
* The system shall allow users to **tap transcript lines to jump** to the exact point in the audio.

### **1.3.2: Transcription & Expert Review** {#1.3.2:-transcription-&-expert-review}

*An expert ensures lectures are accurately transcribed and verified by qualified experts before public release.*

* The system shall transcribe uploaded audio lectures using a **Speech-to-Text (STT) pipeline**  
* The system shall display generated transcripts in the **Admin Panel for expert review**.  
* The Admin Panel shall allow experts to **approve or reject transcripts**.  
* The Admin Panel shall allow experts to **edit transcript text** before approval.  
* Only **approved transcripts** shall be available to end users or used in downstream features (Q\&A, KG, clips).

### **1.3.3: Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations** {#1.3.3:-question-answering-(q&a)-with-citations}

*Allows users to ask natural-language questions and receive precise, citation-backed answers from verified content.*

* The system shall store **approved transcripts** as embeddings in a **Vector Database** for semantic search.  
* The system shall allow users to **submit natural language queries** (in English/Urdu/Arabic).  
* The system shall return answers that are:  
  * **Accurate**, based only on verified transcripts.  
  * **Citation-backed**, with links to Ayah, Hadith, or transcript segments.  
  * **Scoped** to a specific series of lectures  
* The system shall allow users to **tap a citation** to view the transcript segment and play from that timestamp.

### **1.3.4: Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation** {#1.3.4:-knowledge-graph-construction-&-navigation}

*Organizes and links Islamic sources for contextual exploration and reverse lookup.*

* The system shall **extract Ayat, Hadith, and key topics** from approved transcripts.  
* Experts shall **validate extracted entities** before inclusion in the Knowledge Graph.  
* The system shall populate a **Knowledge Graph** linking:  
  * Lectures ↔ Ayat  
  * Lectures ↔ Hadith  
  * Topics ↔ Lectures  
* The system shall allow users to:  
  * View all Ayat/Hadith mentioned in a lecture.  
  * Select an Ayah/Hadith to see **all other lectures that reference it**.

### 

### **1.3.5: Short-Form Content Generation** {#1.3.5:-short-form-content-generation}

*Delivers concise, shareable knowledge through clips derived from long lectures.*

* The system shall automatically propose **30–120 second clips** from transcripts using topic segmentation.  
* Experts shall **review and approve clips** before they are published.  
* The system shall allow users to **play short clips** within the app.  
* The system shall allow users to **share clips** externally (e.g., WhatsApp, social media).

## **1.4 User Classes and Characteristics** {#1.4-user-classes-and-characteristics}

### **1.4.1 Everyday User (Learner)** {#1.4.1-everyday-user-(learner)}

An Everyday User is a Muslim seeking to learn Islam conveniently through audio lectures, short clips, or direct Q\&A. These users may be of varying educational backgrounds and may not have formal Islamic training. They will use the mobile app to browse, listen, and ask questions in natural language. Around 70% of users are expected to access the platform via smartphones, with the remaining using tablets. The system should maintain simplicity, multilingual accessibility (Urdu, English, Arabic), and an intuitive interface for this group. 

### **1.4.2 Student of Knowledge** {#1.4.2-student-of-knowledge}

Students of knowledge are enrolled in Al-Huda programs or other Islamic studies institutions. They require access to authentic sources for research, study, and exam preparation. These users will benefit from features like citation-backed Q\&A, topic linking, and the ability to navigate through transcripts and related Ayat or Hadith. Their average usage frequency will be higher, and they may rely on the semantic search and knowledge graph features extensively.

### **1.4.3 Teacher / Scholar** {#1.4.3-teacher-/-scholar}

Teachers use the platform to quickly locate references and prepare for lectures. They will use the search and citation tools to find specific Ayat, Hadith, and lecture segments. This user class may also provide verified responses or feedback on system-generated answers. Teachers will primarily use the web interface for efficient browsing and research.

### **1.4.4 Expert Reviewer (Al-Huda Panel)** {#1.4.4-expert-reviewer-(al-huda-panel)}

Expert Reviewers are officially appointed scholars or staff from Al-Huda International responsible for verifying system-generated transcriptions, extracted entities, and auto-generated short clips. They will interact primarily through the Admin Panel. Their tasks include editing, approving, or rejecting system outputs to ensure authenticity and accuracy before public release. Training will be provided to familiarize them with the Admin Panel and its workflows.

### **1.4.5 Admin** {#1.4.5-admin}

This group includes the project’s technical maintainers responsible for managing uploads, maintaining the pipeline, fixing bugs, and ensuring system reliability. They will handle lecture ingestion, model deployment, and database monitoring. Admins will also manage expert accounts and system logs via the backend or a restricted admin dashboard. Their role is mostly operational, ensuring smooth end-to-end functioning of the system.

# 

# **2\. Project Requirements**

---

 {#2.-project-requirements}

## **2.1 Use-cases and Use Case Diagrams** {#2.1-use-cases-and-use-case-diagrams}

### **1\) Load Lecture** {#1)-load-lecture}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Load Lecture |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI Admin Panel |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | Sys Admin |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Sys Admin: wants reliable upload and metadata capture. Experts: need audio accessible for review. End Users: eventual access to published lectures. |
| **Preconditions:** | Admin is authenticated & authorized; audio file and basic metadata are available. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Lecture audio and metadata stored on Al Huda media server; transcript generation job queued; |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. Admin opens “Upload Lecture”.  2\. Admin selects audio files and enters metadata (name, speaker, series). 3\. The system validates file format/size and uploads to the media server. 4\. The system registers lecture records and triggers the STT job (Generate Raw Transcript).  5\. The system shows “Upload successful” and places lectures in the “Pending transcription” queue. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Invalid file type → system rejects upload and shows error. 3a. Network error → retry logic; if fails, admin notified. 4a. STT pipeline unavailable → system retries and notifies admin if multiple failures. |

### 

### **2\) Assign Expert** {#2)-assign-expert}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Assign Expert |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI Admin Panel |
| **Level:** | Subfunction |
| **Primary Actor:** | Sys Admin |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Sys Admin: workflows routed correctly. Experts: appropriate assignments. Organization: timely review. |
| **Preconditions:** | The lecture exists and is in “Pending review” / “Pending assignment” state. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | An Expert has an account. And is able to approve/edit/reject transcripts |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. Expert has an account made and is assigned a username and password |
| **Special Requirements:** | Role-based access control (RBAC); audit log entry; notification reliability. |

### **3\) Generate Raw Transcript (automated step included by Load Lecture)** {#3)-generate-raw-transcript-(automated-step-included-by-load-lecture)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Generate Raw Transcript |
| **Scope:** | STT pipeline |
| **Level:** | Subfunction / system |
| **Primary Actor:** | System (STT service) |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Experts/Admins: need accurate raw transcript drafts for review. End Users: rely on verified transcripts only. |
| **Preconditions:** | Audio file uploaded and STT job queued; language(s) selected or auto-detected. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Raw transcript stored (raw\_version); transcript generation job complete; Transcript available on admin panel |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. STT service picks up a lecture job. 2\. Audio processed (language detection if required). 3\. Transcript generated with timestamps and segments.  4\. Transcript saved in DB as draft and linked to lecture; admin/expert notified. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Mixed-language detection → system segments audio by language and runs appropriate STT models. 2b. STT confidence low → mark low-confidence segments for expert attention. 3a. Failure → retry; escalate if repeated. |
| **Special Requirements:** | STT must process 1 hour of audio ≤ 120 min (real-time ×2). Timestamps accuracy such that audio-transcript sync ≤200 ms for playback. Store raw+versioning. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Open source STT models that we self host. Eg OpenAI whisper |

### **4\) Review & Edit Transcript (Approve / Reject)** {#4)-review-&-edit-transcript-(approve-/-reject)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Review and Edit Transcript |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI Admin Panel |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | Expert / Scholar |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | **Expert:** must correct content for accuracy.  **Sys Admin:** needs traceable approvals.  **End User:** only verified transcripts used in downstream features. |
| **Preconditions:** | Draft (raw) transcript exists and Expert is authenticated/authorized. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Transcript is approved (or rejected) with audit trail; approved transcript is immutable and available for downstream use. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. Expert opens lecture transcript.  2\. Expert listens to audio, scrolls synchronized transcript, and edits text segments.  3\. The expert corrects inaccurate segments or adds annotations (Ayah/Hadith flags).  4\. Expert clicks “Approve”.  5\. System records approval with user ID, timestamp, and creates a new approved transcript version (immutable); downstream jobs (KG extraction, embeddings) are triggered. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Expert finds many errors → saves edits as new version but leaves status as Pending. 4a. Expert clicks “Reject” → system records rejection reason and moves lecture back to admin for reassignment or re-STT. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Version history maintained (copy-on-write). Only approved transcripts indexed into Vector DB. All edits & approvals logged. Admin panel loads transcript pages \<4s. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Inline edit UI, side-by-side audio+transcript |

### **5\) Play Audio Lecture (Admin / Expert review)** {#5)-play-audio-lecture-(admin-/-expert-review)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Play Audio Lecture |
| **Scope:** | Admin Panel / Media Server |
| **Level:** | Subfunction |
| **Primary Actor:** | Expert / Sys Admin |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Expert: needs smooth playback while editing; Users: expect reliable playback in app. |
| **Preconditions:** | Lecture audio on the media server; user authorized to access. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Audio plays and remains synchronized with transcript; playback controls work. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. Expert clicks play in the admin panel.  2\. The player streams audio from the media server.  3\. Transcript scrolls in sync; experts can pause/seek/jump to timestamp. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Buffering → system prefetches or reduces bitrate.  2b. Media server not responding → retry or show error. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Sync error \<200 ms; playback buffering \<1s on 4G. |

### **6\) Specify Highlights for Short-Form Content** {#6)-specify-highlights-for-short-form-content}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Specify Highlights for Short-Form Content |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI Admin Panel |
| **Level:** | Subfunction |
| **Primary Actor:** | Expert / Scholar |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Experts: choose pedagogically useful clips. End Users: get concise, accurate short clips. |
| **Preconditions:** | Approved or draft transcript accessible; audio available. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Highlight ranges (start/end timestamps) saved and flagged for clip generation and review. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. An Expert selects text or transcript segments as highlights.  2\. Expert optionally sets clip duration, title, and reason.  3\. System saves highlight metadata and queues clip generation. |
| **Extensions:** | 1a. Expert selects overlapping highlights → system warns and suggests merging. 2a. Expert requests re-segmentation by topic → system re-runs segmentation. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Clips should be 30–120s; highlight metadata includes provenance and verifier. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Manual highlight UI, CSV of highlights, or auto-proposed highlights editable by expert. |

### 

### **7\) Generate Short Form Content From Highlights** {#7)-generate-short-form-content-from-highlights}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Generate Short Clip from Highlights |
| **Scope:** | Backend clip-processing pipeline |
| **Level:** | Subfunction |
| **Primary Actor:** | System (clip generator) |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | Experts/Admins: want clips consistent with highlights. End Users: want shareable clips with relevant content |
| **Preconditions:** | Highlights saved and approved for clip generation. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Clip files created, metadata attached, clip queued for expert approval/publishing. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. System retrieves highlight timestamps and audio.  2\. The system extracts audio segments and generates clip files.  3\. Clip metadata (title, timestamps, source lecture, verifier) stored and presented in the admin panel for final approval. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Clip generation fails → retry; if repeated fail, notify admin.  3a. Expert edits clip (trim/metadata) and approves/rejects. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Average clip generation ≤3 minutes per lecture. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Audio-only |

### **8\) Browse Lectures (End User)** {#8)-browse-lectures-(end-user)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | ----- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Browse Lectures |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App (mobile) |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | **End User:** find lectures by series, speaker, topic.  **Platform:** surface verified content. |
| **Preconditions:** | Network access |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | The user finds and opens a lecture detail page for playback or exploration. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user opens the app.  2.(optional) User applies filters (series, speaker, topic, language).  3\. System returns matching lecture list sorted by relevance/date.  4\. The user taps lecture to open the detail page and listen to lecture. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. Offline mode → show cached list and unavailable label for non-downloaded lectures.  3a. No results → show suggestions or broaden filters. |
| **Special Requirements:** | App startup \<10s; filter operations responsive (\<2s). Support RTL languages. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Search via semantic search or keyword; server-side pagination; local caching. |

### **9\) Play Lecture (End User)** {#9)-play-lecture-(end-user)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Play Lecture |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App \+ Media Server |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | End User: uninterrupted playback, timestamp jump. |
| **Preconditions:** | Lecture available (streamable) or downloaded; |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Lecture plays; transcript sync available; playback position saved for resume. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user opens a lecture and presses Play.  2\. App streams from media server (or plays downloaded file).  3\. Transcript scrolls in sync; the user can pause/seek/adjust speed.  4\. The app periodically saves playback positions. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. User hits Download → system downloads for offline; download stored securely.  2b. Network drops → resume from saved position when possible. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Buffering \<1s; sync ≤200 ms; offline downloads work under unstable networks. |

### **10\) View Synchronized Transcripts (and Jump to Timestamp)** {#10)-view-synchronized-transcripts-(and-jump-to-timestamp)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | View Synchronized Transcripts |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | End User: wants to read and jump to points. Experts: accurate timestamps matter. |
| **Preconditions:** | Approved transcript available and indexed with timestamps. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Users can read transcripts and jump to any segment in audio. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user opens a transcript view with the lecture.  2\. Transcript lines highlight in sync as audio plays.  3\. The user taps a transcript line → player jumps to that timestamp and continues playing. |
| **Extensions:** | 1a. Transcript missing timestamps → show static transcript and “Jump to timestamp” unavailable. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Audio-transcript sync ≤200 ms; transcript must support multiple languages and RTL; tapping a timestamp should respond \<1s. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Inline transcript with clickable timestamps |

### 

### **11\) Ask Natural Language Questions (Q\&A with citations)** {#11)-ask-natural-language-questions-(q&a-with-citations)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Ask Natural Language Questions |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App (Q\&A service \+ Vector DB \+ RAG) |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | **End User:** wants precise, citation-backed answers.  **Platform/Experts:** answers must be limited to verified sources. |
| **Preconditions:** | Vector DB populated with embeddings from approved transcripts; Q\&A API available. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | The user receives an accurate, citation-backed answer with links to source transcript segments/Ayah/Hadith. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user enters a question and optional scope (e.g., specific series).  2\. The system performs semantic search over approved transcripts.  3\. The RAG system composes an answer citing transcript segments and Ayah/Hadith where applicable.  4\. The system presents answers with clickable citations that open lecture/transcript/timestamp. |
| **Extensions:** | 2a. No relevant results → system replies “No verified answer found”.  3a. Ambiguous question → system asks clarifying questions or offers scoped options (series/speaker). |
| **Special Requirements:** | Only verified/approved transcripts used; average query response time \<2s; sanitize inputs to avoid injection; logs of queries stored for auditing. |
| **Technology & Data Variations:** | Queries in English/Urdu/Arabic; scoped to series or global. Answers must include provenance metadata. |

### 

### **12\) View Connected Entities (Ayah/Hadith/Lectures)** {#12)-view-connected-entities-(ayah/hadith/lectures)}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | View Connected Entities |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | End User: explore contextual links between lecture and sources. |
| **Preconditions:** | Entities extracted and approved; KG populated and indexed. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Users can view related Ayat/Hadith and navigate to other lectures referencing them. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user opens a lecture detail and taps “View related content/sources”.  2\. The system displays a list of Ayat/Hadith/Topics with counts and links.  3\. The user taps an entity → system shows all lectures referencing that entity and allows playback at relevant timestamps. |
| **Special Requirements:** | KG queries (3-hop) return results ≤1s; |

### **13\) Play Short Clip & Share Clip** {#13)-play-short-clip-&-share-clip}

| Field | Content |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name:** | Play Short Clip & Share Clip |
| **Scope:** | HudaAI App |
| **Level:** | User goal |
| **Primary Actor:** | End User |
| **Stakeholders & Interests:** | End User: consume and share bite-sized verified content. |
| **Preconditions:** | Approved clip exists and is published. |
| **Success Guarantee (Postcondition):** | Clip plays and can be shared with correct metadata and source citation. |
| **Main Success Scenario:** | 1\. The user opens the clip and taps Play.  2\. Clip streams instantly; playback controls available. 3\. User taps Share → chooses channel (WhatsApp, social).  4\. The system prepares shared payload including clip link, thumbnail, source lecture, timestamp, and verifier info. |
| **Special Requirements:** | Clip playback buffer \<1s; shared payload includes immutable provenance and link to the transcript/timestamp. |

## 

| Note: Our system comprises two use case diagrams due to the distinct scopes of the admin panel, and the mobile application. |
| :---- |

## **2.1.1 Admin Panel Use Case Diagram** {#2.1.1-admin-panel-use-case-diagram}

**![][image1]**

## 

## **2.1.2 App Use Case Diagram** {#2.1.2-app-use-case-diagram}

**![][image2]**

## 

## **2.2 Functional Requirements** {#2.2-functional-requirements}

### **2.2.1. Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts** {#2.2.1.-lecture-discovery,-playback-&-interactive-transcripts}

* The system shall allow users to **browse and search** lectures by series, topic, or speaker.  
* The system shall provide **audio playback controls** — play, pause, rewind, and fast-forward.  
* The system shall allow users to **download full lectures** for offline playback.  
* The system shall display **time-synchronized transcripts** beside the audio player.  
* The system shall allow users to **tap transcript lines** to jump to the exact point in audio.  
* The system shall maintain playback position for resumed sessions.  
* The system shall support **multi-language interface and transcript display** (Urdu, Arabic, English).

### **2.2.2. Transcription & Expert Review** {#2.2.2.-transcription-&-expert-review}

* The system shall **ingest uploaded lecture audio** and automatically transcribe it using an STT pipeline.  
* The system shall **store both raw and reviewed transcripts**.  
* The Admin Panel shall display **transcription drafts** for expert review.  
* Experts shall be able to **edit transcript text** before approval.  
* Experts shall be able to **approve or reject** a transcript.  
* Only **approved transcripts** shall be published or used for downstream features (Q\&A, Knowledge Graph, Clips).  
* The system shall maintain **version history** of transcript edits.  
* The system shall notify admins when new transcriptions are ready for review.

### **2.2.3. Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations** {#2.2.3.-question-answering-(q&a)-with-citations}

* The system shall **store approved transcripts as embeddings** in a Vector Database.  
* The system shall allow users to **submit questions** in natural language (English, Urdu, or Arabic).  
* The system shall perform **semantic search** over verified transcripts to find relevant answers.  
* The system shall **generate precise, citation-backed answers** linked to verified sources.  
* The system shall display **citations** with links to Ayah, Hadith, or transcript segments.  
* The system shall allow users to **tap citations** to open the transcript and play the related audio.  
* The system shall **scope answers** to a specific lecture series or teacher when specified by the user.  
* The system shall **log all user queries** and store them for performance monitoring and future improvement.

### **2.2.4. Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation** {#2.2.4.-knowledge-graph-construction-&-navigation}

* The system shall automatically **extract entities** (Ayat, Hadith, key topics) from approved transcripts.  
* Experts shall **validate and approve** extracted entities before inclusion.  
* The system shall **link entities** within a Knowledge Graph:  
  * Lectures ↔ Ayat  
  * Lectures ↔ Hadith  
  * Topics ↔ Lectures  
* The system shall allow users to **view all Ayat/Hadith mentioned** in a lecture.  
* The system shall allow users to **select an Ayah or Hadith** to see all lectures referencing it.  
* The system shall support **reverse navigation** from any node (Ayah/Hadith) to connected content.  
* The system shall **visualize relationships** between lectures, Ayah, and topics.

### 

### **2.2.5. Short-Form Content Generation** {#2.2.5.-short-form-content-generation}

* The system shall automatically **segment transcripts** into short 30–120 s clips using topic detection.  
* The system shall allow experts to **review, edit, and approve clips** before publication.  
* The system shall allow users to **play clips** directly within the app.  
* The system shall allow users to **share clips externally** (e.g., WhatsApp, social media).  
* The system shall **display source metadata** for each clip (lecture title, timestamp, and expert verifier).  
* The system shall store **engagement metrics** (views, shares, likes) for analytics.

## 

## **2.3 Non-Functional Requirements** {#2.3-non-functional-requirements}

### **2.3.1 Reliability** {#2.3.1-reliability}

**Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts**

* The system shall provide consistent playback without crashes or stutters across supported devices.  
* The app shall automatically resume playback from the last saved position after interruption or app closure.  
* Offline playback and downloads shall work reliably even under unstable or slow network conditions.

**Transcription & Expert Review**

* The transcription service shall handle network or model failures with automatic retries and error logging.  
* Approved transcripts shall remain accessible and immutable once published.  
* The system shall maintain backup copies of raw and verified transcripts for recovery in case of failure.

**Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations**

* The Q\&A service shall only return results derived from verified and approved transcripts.  
* In case of retrieval failure, the system shall gracefully inform the user and retry the query.  
* Logs of user queries and answer generations shall be retained for audit and debugging.

**Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation**

* The Knowledge Graph shall maintain referential integrity between all connected entities (Lecture ↔ Ayah/Hadith ↔ Topic).  
* Graph data shall be periodically validated to prevent broken or orphaned links.

**Short-Form Content Generation**

* Approved clips shall remain accessible even if the parent lecture is archived.  
* The system shall retry failed clip processing jobs automatically.

### **2.3.2 Usability** {#2.3.2-usability}

**Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts**

* The interface shall be intuitive, clean, and responsive for mobile users.  
* Transcripts shall scroll automatically with the audio playback and be visually distinguishable by timestamp.  
* The user shall be able to easily switch between languages (Urdu, Arabic, English).

**Transcription & Expert Review**

* The Admin Panel shall provide a user-friendly review interface with side-by-side comparison and edit tracking.  
* Experts shall have access to filters for “Pending”, “Approved”, and “Rejected” transcripts.  
* Visual indicators shall clearly show approval status and version history.

**Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations**

* Answers shall be displayed with clear formatting, source citations, and clickable timestamps.  
* The query input shall support right-to-left script and multilingual typing.  
* Error or “no results” states shall be explained clearly with suggestions.

**Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation**

* The Knowledge Graph interface shall allow smooth zooming, panning, and clickable nodes for navigation.  
* Users shall easily identify relationships between lectures, Ayat, Hadith, and topics.

**Short-Form Content Generation**

* Clips shall load quickly with visual thumbnails and simple share buttons.  
* The sharing interface shall use recognizable social icons and minimal steps.

### **2.3.3 Performance** {#2.3.3-performance}

**Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts**

* Audio playback synchronization delay with transcript shall not exceed **200 ms**.  
* App startup time shall be under 10 **seconds** on average devices.

**Transcription & Expert Review**

* The STT pipeline shall process one hour of audio within **real-time × 2** (≤ 120 minutes).  
* The Admin Panel shall load transcript pages in **under 4 seconds**.

**Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations**

* Average query response time shall be **\< 2 seconds** under normal load.  
* The Vector Database shall support concurrent semantic searches for **100+ users** without degradation.

**Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation**

* Entity lookups and 3-hop traversals shall return results within **1 second**.  
* Graph updates (new nodes/edges) shall propagate within **30 seconds** of expert approval.

**Short-Form Content Generation**

* Automatic clip generation for a lecture shall complete within **3 minutes average**.  
* Clip playback buffering shall be **\< 1 second** on standard 4G networks.

---

### **2.3.4 Security** {#2.3.4-security}

**Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts**

* User data (downloads, preferences) shall be stored securely using encrypted local storage.  
* All client-server communication shall use **HTTPS/TLS 1.3**.

**Transcription & Expert Review**

* Only authenticated and authorized experts shall access the Admin Panel.  
* Role-based access control (RBAC) shall distinguish between Admins, Experts, and View-Only roles.  
* All transcript edits and approvals shall be logged with timestamps and user IDs.

**Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations**

* Only verified content shall be indexed in the Vector Database.  
* The Q\&A module shall prevent injection or malicious query input via sanitization and rate-limiting.  
* Model responses shall be filtered to avoid any generated or unverifiable religious content.

**Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation**

* Access to modify or delete KG entities shall be restricted to authorized experts.  
* Every edge in the KG shall record provenance (who created or verified it).

**Short-Form Content Generation**

* Only approved clips shall be exposed through public endpoints.

## 

## **2.4 Domain Model**  {#2.4-domain-model}

 

![][image3]

 

# 

# **3\. System Overview**

---

 {#3.-system-overview}

## **3.1 Architectural Design** {#3.1-architectural-design}

### **High Level Architecture Diagram** {#high-level-architecture-diagram}

**![][image4]**

### 

### **Conceptual Diagram** {#conceptual-diagram}

**![][image5]**

## 

## **3.2 Design Models** {#3.2-design-models}

### **Class Diagram** {#class-diagram}

**![][image6]**

 

## **3.3 Data Design** {#3.3-data-design}

The information domain of HudaAI—comprising Islamic audio lectures, transcripts, and semantic knowledge—is systematically transformed into structured data through an AI-driven processing pipeline. All raw lecture audio files are first stored on the **Al-Huda Media Server**, which acts as the primary file repository. These audio files are fetched by the system and passed through the **Speech-to-Text (STT) pipeline**, where multilingual transcription models convert them into raw text segments with timestamps. The resulting raw transcriptions are stored in a **relational database** along with associated metadata such as lecturer, series, duration, and language. Within the **Admin Panel**, experts and scholars can access these transcriptions, make corrections, and either approve or reject them. The edited and verified transcripts are then stored back in the same relational database, maintaining version history for transparency and auditability.

Once a transcript is approved, it undergoes further processing and enrichment. The verified text is **embedded** into numerical vectors and stored in a **Vector Database** to enable semantic search and the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) question-answering system. In parallel, **semantic annotation** is applied to extract entities such as *Ayahs*, *Hadiths*, and *Topics*, which are organized and interlinked in a **Knowledge Graph (graph database)**. This structure allows contextual linking between different lectures and Islamic sources. On the user-facing side, the **mobile app retrieves** audio files directly from the Al-Huda Media Server, transcript data from the relational database, related entities from the Knowledge Graph, and AI-generated responses from the Vector Database. Users can stream or download lectures, view synchronized transcripts, and explore conceptually connected knowledge.

**Data Storage List:**

* **File Server (Media Server):** Stores all audio lecture files.  
* **Relational Database (e.g., PostgreSQL):** Stores metadata, raw and approved transcripts, user data, and version history.  
* **Vector Database:** Stores embeddings of verified transcripts for semantic search and RAG.  
* **Knowledge Graph :** Stores interlinked entities (Ayah, Hadith, Topic, Lecture relationships).
